description: List all schedules in a project. Use the Show Project API when you need the project time zone to interpret UTC timestamps or values expressed in project time.

parameters.name: filters[schedule_type]
parameters.description: Filter schedules by type. Values must exactly match a valid schedule type (case-sensitive)

parameters.name: filters[updated_at][gt]
parameters.description: Filter for schedules updated after this timestamp (ISO 8601 instant)

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort by supported fields. Accepts comma separated values to sort by multiple fields. Order is ascending by default, prefix field with '-' for descending

schedule_id.description: The unique identifier for the schedule

schedule_type.description: Indicates whether the schedule type is project schedule or lookahead

is_active.description: Flag to indicate whether the schedule is active or archived

data_date.description: Datetime string when the schedule's progress was last recorded in UTC ISO-8601 format

start_date.description: The schedule's start date as a datetime string in UTC ISO-8601 format

parent_schedule_id.description: The schedule_id of the associated project schedule if this schedule is a lookahead

updated_at.description: The date and time the schedule was last modified in UTC

updated_by.description: The user ID of the user who last modified the schedule

created_at.description: The date and time the schedule was created in UTC

created_by.description: The user ID of the user who created the schedule

deleted_at.description: The date and time the schedule was deleted in UTC

deleted_by.description: The user ID of the user who deleted the schedule

400.description: The server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax or parameters that fail to meet validation requirements.

401.description: Although the HTTP standard specifies "unauthorized", semantically this response means "unauthenticated". That is, the client must authenticate itself to get the requested response.

403.description: The client does not have access rights to the content; that is, it is authenticated, but the server is refusing to give the requested resource. Unlike 401, the client's identity is known to the server.

404.description: The server can not find the requested resource. This can mean either the URL is not recognized, or the resource itself does not exist. Servers may also send this response instead of 403 to hide the existence of a resource from an unauthorized client.
